  • Changelog

    Module: cor_logistic

    module-cor-logistic.Rmd

    Purpose

    cor_logistic fits logistic regression models for binary correlates of risk. It is currently designed for HPV vaccine trials where the endpoint is binary (infected / not infected) rather than time-to-event.

    Source module: correlates_reporting2/cor_logistic/
    Key reports: report.Rmd, report_by_COR.Rmd

    Note on data

    The HPV trial data (id27hpv, id27hpvnAb) are not publicly distributed in the mock data files shipped with this package. The workflow below shows how to point the session at your own copy of the processed data.

    # Point to your local copy of the HPV trial data
hpv_data_dir <- "/path/to/your/hpv_data_clean"

    Reproduce the report

    1. Configure the session 

    library(utilrep2)

setup_session(
  trial       = "id27hpv",
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = hpv_data_dir,
  report_root = "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
)

    2. Run the module 

    run_cor_logistic(trial = "id27hpv")

    For id27hpv this runs cor_logistic.R for M18 and M18sus. For id27hpvnAb it runs the same script for M18nAb and M18susnAb.

    3. Build the chapter PDF 

    build_chapter_report("cor_logistic", trial = "id27hpv")

    Output

    Results are saved under cor_logistic/output/<trial>/.

    Report sections

    • Odds ratio tables per assay and timepoint
    • Marginalized risk curves
    • Forest plots across marker definitions

    Supported trials

    Trial CoR timepoints
    id27hpv M18, M18sus
    id27hpvnAb M18nAb, M18susnAb

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
